patch.value: Separate the normal development changelog drift contract from prepared-release validation. Keep the normal CI command unchanged; add an explicit release-context check used by both release preparation and tag publication, then prove the mutation path without pushing.
Validate prepared release changelog in release context
Production Auto Release generated the dated changelog correctly, then the normal unreleased-mode changelog check rejected it before commit, tag, or publication.

Prepared release validation checks CHANGELOG.md with the target release version and retains every remaining release gate; Tag-triggered publication uses the same release-context check; A non-pushing production-path integration proof creates the expected release commit and tag
GitHub Actions Auto Release run 29856125639 on merged master 1b971187
Dispatch Auto Release with push=true and dry_run=false; preparation creates the dated section, then bun run verify:release invokes the unreleased-mode changelog check and exits before commit.
The prepared dated changelog is validated with --release-version and the pipeline proceeds through all gates to commit, tag, and publication.
The prepared dated changelog was compared against unreleased-mode generator output and rejected; no release refs or registry package were created.
The first automatic publication was safely blocked before any partial release state.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Live production run 29856125639 failed safely at changelog verification after version and changelog preparation. Logs contain no exposed credential values,and npm plus GitHub have no 2026.7.21 release artifact.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: No tag or registry artifact existed after the failed run,so retry remains safe after a merged fix.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: A generated release changelog and an unreleased development changelog are two valid projections of the same pm data and must be checked with matching generator context.

value.note: Separate normal and prepared release gate composition
Validate generated changelog in target-version context
Use prepared release context at the tag

author: codex-auto-release
message: Keep closed feature as related lineage without active-child lifecycle conflict

value.note: Preserve historical headings and item evidence in both unreleased and prepared projections

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: The first isolated proof invocation was launched from the source checkout instead of the clone and was immediately corrected by regenerating the development changelog from pm data. It also showed the preservation guard incorrectly required the development-only Unreleased heading; the guard now enforces historical headings plus all evidence item links in either valid projection.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Proof clone: /tmp/monica-prepared-release-proof.L5HSnu. This path is local evidence only and contains no credentials or pushed refs.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Isolated non-pushing production-path proof passed in a fresh clone: target 2026.7.21 was prepared, release-context changelog validation passed, all 547 tests and every release gate passed, npx and bunx both reported 2026.7.21, history audits passed, and a clean v2026.7.21 release commit/tag was created only inside the clone.

patch.value: Normal development changelog validation remains unchanged; prepared release and tag workflows validate with the explicit target version; a clean isolated mutation-path proof created v2026.7.21 after all gates.
Separated reusable release gates from changelog projection checks and applied explicit release-version validation during preparation and tag publication; made preservation projection-neutral.
Both contexts are separated and an isolated production path created a clean v2026.7.21 commit/tag after all gates, including npm and Bun smoke tests.
Prepared release and tag validation use the target release version while normal CI retains unreleased-mode drift checking.
Prepared release validation now uses the target-version projection and the full non-pushing production path passes in isolation.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: The release runbook now documents the separate Unreleased and dated release projections, the commands used for each, and the shared gate suite.
